layer_1_flower.lineage: Face Melt (Grapefruit Chem X THC Bomb) X Governmint Oasis

layer_2_essential_oil.blend_rationale: Lemongrass carries the dominant myrcene. Black Pepper matches the grapefruit aroma note. Sage provides α-Humulene support. Ylang Ylang rounds the blend with a soft finish.

layer_3_music.spotify_prompt: A mid-energy session. Buzzy grapefruit euphoria dissolving stress. Genres: indie rock, neo-soul, jazz, alternative. Tempo around 85-110 BPM.

layer_4_tea.name: Toronja Tea
layer_4_tea.description: Grapefruit and pastry dough infused oolong blend
method: Heat 8 oz filtered water to 195°F.
Add oolong tea bag and steep for 3-4 minutes.
Add fresh grapefruit peel and let infuse for 1 minute.
Add vanilla extract and let infuse for 1 minute.
Strain into a warm teacup.
Stir in honey and serve.

mocktail.name: Toronja Fizz
method: Fill a rocks glass with large ice cubes.
Combine 2 oz fresh grapefruit juice and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker with ice.
Add 0.5 oz vanilla simple syrup.
Shake for 8 seconds.
Strain into glass over fresh ice.
Top with 2 oz sparkling water and stir gently.
Garnish with grapefruit.

cocktail.name: Toronja Spritz
cocktail.description: Gin-based grapefruit and pastry dough cocktail
method: Combine 2 oz gin, 1.5 oz fresh grapefruit juice, and 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice in a shaker.
Add 0.5 oz vanilla simple syrup.
Add ice.
Add ice and shake for 10 seconds.
Strain into a chilled highball.
Garnish with grapefruit.

juice.name: Toronja Juice
method: Press 2 cups fresh grapefruit through a cold press.
Press 1 green apple through the press.
Add 0.5 inch fresh ginger root to the press.
Combine all juices in a glass.
Add 0.5 oz raw honey and stir until dissolved.
Pour over ice and serve immediately.

wellness_shot.name: Toronja Shot
method: Chill a shot glass in the freezer for 2 minutes.
In a mixing glass, combine 1 oz gin with 0.75 oz fresh grapefruit juice.
Add 0.25 oz fresh lemon juice.
Stir with ice for 5 seconds.
Strain into the chilled shot glass and serve immediately.

snack.name: Spiced Cashews with Lime & Chili
snack.desc: Roasted cashews with lime zest, chili flakes, and smoked paprika.

appetizer.name: Ahi Tuna Poke Bowl
appetizer.desc: Fresh ahi tuna with avocado, sesame, ponzu, and crispy shallots.

main.name: Pan-Seared Salmon with Herb Butter
main.desc: Wild-caught salmon with compound butter of dill, lemon, and capers over wilted greens.

dessert.name: Lemon Olive Oil Cake
dessert.desc: Moist cake with Meyer lemon, local olive oil, and a dusting of powdered sugar.

cheese.name: Fresh Mozzarella & Heirloom Tomatoes
cheese.desc: Hand-pulled mozzarella with heirloom tomatoes, basil oil, and aged balsamic.

bath_bomb.base: baking soda, citric acid, cornstarch, Epsom salt
bath_bomb.extras: dried flower petals, oat milk powder

shower_steamer.activation: Place on shower floor away from direct stream. Steam activates essential oils for 5-10 minutes of aromatherapy.

layer_10_incense.burn_method: Place on heat-resistant surface in a ventilated room. Allow 30 seconds for the flame to establish before extinguishing to a smolder.
layer_10_incense.ritual_suggestion: Light before the session begins. Let the smoke settle the room while you prepare your buzzy space.

layer_11_environment.spatial_setup: Mixed seating heights, conversation-friendly arrangement. Ambient background music visible.
layer_11_environment.scent_anchor: Essential oil blend via diffuser, started 25 minutes before session

layer_12_breathwork.timing: Begin with the first sip. Alternate breathing cycles with mindful tasting.
layer_12_breathwork.guidance_text: Find your center. Breathe in through the nose for four counts. Hold for four, noticing the stillness. Exhale slowly through the mouth for six counts, letting the breath carry tension away.

layer_13_narrative.text:
22 drops of lemongrass carry the dominant Myrcene through the room steadily — the way Malama The Roots intended when they cultivated Toronja in Potter Valley, Mendocino County. The lineage reads Face Melt (Grapefruit Chem X THC Bomb) X Governmint Oasis, but what arrives in the glass is something that transcends parentage: grapefruit, pastry dough, and black pepper.

The second oil — Black Pepper — adds 11 drops of brightness that matches the pastry dough note in the flower itself. Buzzy is not a command here but an invitation, the way the best strains suggest rather than insist. The body balances into stress-free with the kind of molecular precision that only terpenes at 1.14% total can deliver.

In the warm gathering space, the essential oil blend has been working for thirty minutes before the flower arrives. The pre-load creates a terpene environment that the cannabis completes rather than initiates — your olfactory system already tuned to the frequency of Myrcene, already primed for buzzy grapefruit euphoria dissolving stress. The third oil adds depth. The fourth oil rounds everything into a finish that feels inevitable rather than constructed.

This is what Malama The Roots grows in Potter Valley, Mendocino County: not just cannabis, but context. The hybrid genetics express through terpenes that speak the same chemical language as rosemary in a Mendocino garden. The session balances into its final form — euphoric, unhurried, complete — and the 4 oils that started it are now indistinguishable from the flower that finished it.

layer_14_location.terpene_walk: Begin at Mount Tamalpais State Park, diffuse essential oil blend 30 minutes before arrival. Walk slowly, breathing deeply through the nose to layer forest phytoncides over the pre-loaded terpene profile.

wine.terpene_bridge: Petite Sirah's dense, jammy character mirrors myrcene's heavy, sedating quality — both deliver weight and body.
Grenache's lush fruit and herbaceous undertones complement myrcene's tropical warmth.

beer.terpene_bridge: Citra and Simcoe hops are myrcene-dominant — cannabis and hops are Cannabaceae cousins sharing the same terpene synthesis pathways.
beer.tasting_note: Tropical citrus, pine resin, clean bitter finish

beer.terpene_bridge: EKG hops carry significant myrcene, connecting malt-forward English ales to cannabis terpene profiles.

layer_15_wine_beer.cannabaceae_note: Cannabis and hops (Humulus lupulus) are botanical cousins in the family Cannabaceae, sharing terpene synthesis pathways that produce identical aromatic molecules.
